features.description: Investigate original issue #117 and automation failure context
features.details: Review issue #117 to understand what the automation was attempting to process when the failure occurred. Document the expected workflow and identify what operation triggered the 'head' attribute access.

features.description: Locate the code accessing PullRequest.head attribute
features.details: Located the problematic code in 'automation/pull_request_handler.py'. The code attempts to access the 'head' attribute directly on a PullRequest object, which is the source of the AttributeError.

Examine the PullRequest class definition or the GitHub API response structure to determine the correct attribute name for accessing head branch information. This may be 'head_ref', 'head_branch', or require accessing a nested object.
Update the code to use the correct attribute name or proper object structure for accessing the pull request head information. Ensure the fix handles the data correctly.

features.description: Add defensive error handling for attribute access
features.details: Implement try-except blocks or hasattr() checks around attribute access on PullRequest objects to provide more informative error messages and prevent similar crashes in the future.

features.description: Add unit tests for PullRequest attribute access
features.details: Create unit tests that verify correct handling of PullRequest objects, including tests for the head attribute access and edge cases where attributes may be missing.

features.description: Reprocess issue #117 and close the failure report
features.details: After the fix is deployed, manually trigger reprocessing of issue #117 to verify the fix works correctly. Close this issue #121 once successful processing is confirmed.

overall_approach: Investigate the automation codebase to locate where the 'head' attribute is being accessed on a PullRequest object, determine the correct attribute name or data structure, implement the fix, and add proper error handling to prevent similar failures
potential_risks: The PullRequest object structure may vary depending on GitHub API version or context
Other parts of the automation may have similar attribute access issues
Fixing this may reveal additional underlying issues with issue #117 processing
Without access to the original issue #117, the full scope of the problem may not be apparent
